tool.name: auto_queue_cve_exploits
tool.description:
Automatically queue CVE-based exploitation hypotheses from fingerprinted tech stack.
This is the MAIN INTEGRATION FUNCTION that bridges: - Tech stack detection (identify_tech_stack) - CVE lookup (version_to_cves) - Hypothesis queueing (hypothesis_ledger)
WORKFLOW: 1. Parse tech_stack dictionary for products with versions 2. Call version_to_cves() for each technology 3. Map CVEs to vulnerability classes (sqli, xss, rce, path_traversal, etc.) 4. Generate attack surfaces based on product/CVE combination 5. Queue hypotheses in hypothesis ledger 6. Return prioritized exploitation plan
WHEN TO USE: - After calling identify_tech_stack() and finding versioned software - When you want to automatically discover and queue CVE exploits - To transform fingerprinting results into actionable attacks
ELITE FEATURE: - Automatically prioritizes CVEs with available exploits (Metasploit, PoC) - Generates product-specific attack surfaces (e.g., Apache path traversal endpoints) - Recommends tailored payloads based on CVE descriptions - Prevents hypothesis flooding with max_hypotheses limit

parameter.name: tech_stack
base_url
parameter.description:
Target base URL for generating attack surfaces.
Examples: - "https://example.com" - "http://192.168.1.100:8080" - "https://api.target.com"
Attack surfaces are built relative to this URL (e.g., "https://example.com/cgi-bin/.%2e/.%2e/etc/passwd" for Apache path traversal)

parameter.name: hypothesis_ledger
parameter.description:
HypothesisLedger instance for direct integration.
If provided, hypotheses are queued directly via hypothesis_ledger.add(). If not provided, falls back to calling add_hypothesis() tool.
Recommended: Pass the ledger instance for better performance.

parameter.name: min_severity
parameter.description:
Minimum CVE severity to queue.
Valid values: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW
Examples: - "CRITICAL" → Only queue critical CVEs (CVSS 9.0+) - "HIGH" → Queue critical and high CVEs (CVSS 7.0+) - "MEDIUM" → Queue medium+ severity (CVSS 4.0+)
Recommendation: Use "MEDIUM" for comprehensive testing, "HIGH" for targeted attacks.

parameter.name: prioritize_exploits
parameter.description:
If true, prioritize CVEs with available exploits (Metasploit, PoC).
Exploitation plan will list CVEs with exploits first, regardless of severity. This is recommended for penetration testing where exploitability matters more than theoretical severity.

parameter.name: max_hypotheses
parameter.description:
Maximum number of hypotheses to queue.
Prevents hypothesis flooding when many CVEs are found. Only the top-priority hypotheses (by severity + exploit availability) are queued.
Recommended values: - 20 → Balanced testing - 10 → Focused on highest-priority CVEs only - 50 → Comprehensive testing (may take longer)

returns.description: Dictionary with exploitation plan and queuing summary: { "hypotheses_queued": 5, "cves_found": 12, "exploitation_plan": [ { "priority": 1, "cve_id": "CVE-2021-41773", "product": "Apache/2.4.49", "severity": "CRITICAL", "vuln_class": "path_traversal", "hypothesis_id": "H-0042", "attack_surface": "https://example.com/cgi-bin/.%2e/.%2e/etc/passwd", "exploit_available": true, "exploit_type": "metasploit", "exploit_url": "https://www.exploit-db.com/exploits/50383", "confidence": "HIGH", "recommended_payloads": ["../.../../etc/passwd", ".%2e/.%2e/etc/passwd"], "recommended_action": "Deploy Metasploit module immediately - CRITICAL severity RCE" }, ... ], "summary": "Queued 5 CVE exploits: 2 CRITICAL, 3 HIGH", "status": "success" }

example.scenario: Apache 2.4.49 with known path traversal CVE

security_notes.note: This function ONLY queries public CVE databases - no interaction with target
Hypothesis queueing is local - does not send payloads to target
Rate limiting applied to NVD/ExploitDB queries (inherited from version_to_cves)

integration_notes.note: Designed to be called immediately after identify_tech_stack()
Works seamlessly with existing hypothesis_ledger and payload generation
Exploitation plan can be fed directly to agent's attack loop

tool.name: enrich_hypothesis_with_cve
tool.description:
Enrich an existing hypothesis with CVE metadata.
Useful when CVEs are discovered AFTER hypothesis creation (e.g., manual research). Fetches full CVE details from NVD and stores them in hypothesis metadata.
WHEN TO USE: - You've created a hypothesis and later discovered a relevant CVE - You want to add CVE context to manual hypotheses - You need CVE details for reporting confirmed vulnerabilities

parameter.name: hypothesis_id
parameter.description:
Hypothesis ID to enrich (e.g., "H-0042").
Must be an existing hypothesis in the ledger.

parameter.name: cve_id
parameter.description:
CVE identifier (e.g., "CVE-2021-41773").
Must be valid CVE format: CVE-YYYY-NNNNN

parameter.name: hypothesis_ledger
parameter.description:
HypothesisLedger instance.
If provided, CVE data is stored directly in hypothesis.metadata.

tool.name: get_cve_exploitation_status
tool.description:
Check if a CVE has been tested and get exploitation status.
Searches hypothesis ledger for hypotheses linked to a specific CVE. Returns whether the CVE is exploitable, testing in progress, or not tested.
WHEN TO USE: - Before queueing a CVE exploit (check if already tested) - For reporting exploitation success rates - To avoid redundant CVE testing

returns.description:
{ "cve_id": "CVE-2021-41773", "tested": true, "confirmed": true, "hypotheses": ["H-0042", "H-0043"], "status": "EXPLOITABLE", "summary": "CVE-2021-41773 is exploitable" }
Status values: - "EXPLOITABLE" → CVE confirmed exploitable - "TESTING_IN_PROGRESS" → Hypotheses exist but not confirmed - "NOT_TESTED" → No hypotheses found - "ERROR" → Failed to check status
